demographics.living_situation: 4-month-old infant at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Milk keeps pouring out of his nose when he tries to drink, and his diapers have been completely dry since yesterday afternoon. (Reported by the infant's parent.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Our 4-month-old baby has refused to feed for a whole day, keeps bringing milk back up through his nose, is burning up with a fever, and hasn't had a wet nappy since yesterday. He's gone really floppy and sleepy and we're frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: feeding became very difficult about 24 hours ago; a high fever came on about 6 hours ago
symptom_narrative.character: when we try to feed him he cries hard, pushes the special bottle away, and milk comes straight back out of his nose; now he is floppy and very sleepy with a weak, hoarse cry
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has ever been — he just won't take anything
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: he refuses every feed and it gets worse each time we try
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped; we have tried several different special teats and bottles
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to feed him makes him cry and the milk comes out of his nose
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: hot with fever, very sleepy and floppy, no wet nappy since yesterday afternoon (about 14 hours), no tears when he cries, and his mouth looks dry
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has always been a bit hard to feed and has been slow to put on weight because of his cleft
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is not feeding at all and is far too sleepy

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: born with a cleft lip and a cleft palate that have not been repaired yet
past_medical_history.how_managed: feeds with special cleft bottles/teats; under the care of the cleft team and waiting for surgical repair

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: we gave him a dose about 4 hours ago but it barely helped

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: fully dependent 4-month-old infant cared for at home by two exhausted and very anxious parents

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: historically slow weight gain attributed by the parents to feeding difficulty from the cleft

objective_data_offered.value: 39.1 degrees C measured on our home thermometer
objective_data_offered.timing: about 6 hours ago and still hot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home thermometer reading taken by anxious parents

objective_data_offered.value: no wet nappy since yesterday afternoon, about 14 hours
milk pours back out of his nose when he tries to drink and he refuses the bottle
he is very sleepy and floppy and his cry is weak and hoarse, not his usual self
his skin looks blotchy and mottled and he feels hot to touch
no tears when he cries and his mouth looks dry and sticky
